---
title: "inat_sex_annotations"
---

```{r}

library(dplyr)

d_sex_known <- readRDS("_targets/objects/d_sex") %>% filter(sex == "female" | sex == "male")

# Define the path to the directory
image_directory <- "C:/Users/hiest/Desktop/odonate_sex_vetting_john"
# List all image files in the directory with ".jpg" extension
image_files <- list.files(path = image_directory, pattern = "\\.jpg$", full.names = FALSE)
# Display the vector of image files
image_files <- sub("\\.jpg$", "", image_files)

d_sex_known$img_id

d_sex_known <- d_sex_known %>%
  filter(img_id %in% image_files)

d_sex_known <- select(d_sex_known,c("img_id","sex"))

d_sex_known <- d_sex_known %>%
  distinct(img_id, .keep_all = TRUE)

install.packages("clipr")
library(clipr)
write_clip(d_sex_known)

random_rows <- sample_n(d_sex_known, 500)
random_rows$img_id

sampled_df <- d_sex_known %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  slice_sample(n = 3) %>%
  ungroup()

sampled_df$img_id

python_list <- paste0('["', paste(sampled_df$img_id, collapse = '","'), '"]')

# Print the result
cat(python_list)

test <- colorEvoHelpers::readDarwincoreDF("C:/Users/hiest/Desktop/Absolutely Key Papers/dwc.zip")
colnames(test)
test

test$
  
View(d)

sampled_df$img_id

#pes_c515 - 21000
#ted_goshulak - 25 
# kcarrol2 - 503
# joebartok - 42000
# msavard - 73
# adam kalab - 16000 
# peterlane - 1700 
# bgooding 66000
# bgooding
# steve collins 2005
# smith - 1700 
# bertharris - 600
# briangooding
# orichen10- 498
# joeseph22 - 3501
# colindjones - 9000
# pmanphoto - 168 
# ronlys - 5000
# kalab
# bgooding 

library(ggplot2)
# check records metrics
d2 <- read.csv("data/balanced_records_metrics.csv")

balanced <- read.csv
species_summary$species
species_summary <- d2 %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarize(observations = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(observations))

species_summary <- dplyr::filter(species_summary, observations > 2)

ggplot(species_summary, aes(x = observations)) +  # Log transformation
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10) +  # Adjust binwidth for clarity
  labs(title = "Log-Scaled Histogram of Species Observation Counts",
       x = "Number of observations", y = "Number of species") +
  xlim(0,200)

nrow(species_summary)


cor.test(test$mean_saturation, test$latitude)
d2$mean_saturation

test <- d2 %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarize(mean_red = mean(mean_red),mean_green=mean(mean_green),mean_blue=mean(mean_blue),
            mean_saturation=mean(mean_saturation),mean_value=mean(mean_value),latitude=mean(latitude))


```
